answer:
**In Charles Comte's *Traité de Législation*, Volume II, Chapter 7 ("Of the physical development acquired, in America and in the islands of the great Ocean, under different degrees of latitude, by peoples of the American, Malay, and Negro species or varieties"), the "développement physique acquis en Amérique" refers to the acquired physical development—specifically stature, bodily proportions, strength, robustness, and overall constitution—of various Indigenous American peoples (primarily of the "American" or "copper-colored" species) observed across a wide range of latitudes.**[^1]

Comte critiques Montesquieu's theory that cold climates produce larger, stronger bodies while heat produces weaker ones, using traveler accounts to show the opposite pattern in America: physical development is often *stronger* in temperate and tropical zones than in polar or subpolar ones.[^2] Key examples include:

- **High northern latitudes (cold climates)**: Eskimos near 70°N (Copper River) are small (~4 feet), poorly made, and weak; those at Hudson Bay ~4 feet; Mackenzie River Indians thin, small, scabby; Bering Bay and Nootka (49°N) weak-framed.[^3]
- **Temperate mid-latitudes**: Canadian natives (45–50°N, e.g., Iroquois, Hurons) tall and well-made but low corpulence; Monterrey Indians (36°N) robust and skilled.[^4]
- **Near-equatorial tropics**: Louisiana natives strong and indefatigable; Mbayas/Lenguas (20–22°S) ~5'8"–5'9", beautifully proportioned; Caribs (8–10°S, equator) athletic and energetic (rowing 14–15 hours in 30° Réaumur heat); Amazon/equator Indians equally strong; Mexican Tenateros carry 225–350 lbs in mines under high heat.[^5]
- **High southern latitudes (cold)**: Tierra del Fuego (52–53°S) small, thin, miserable; contrasted with tall, strong Patagonians (~45°S, up to 6'7") and Tehuelche nomads nearby.[^6]

Comte notes exceptions (e.g., some small equatorial tribes) but argues variations stem more from species/race, industry, land fertility, and subsistence than climate alone, with stronger physiques near the equator.[^7] The chapter transitions to Pacific islands for comparison before concluding climate's influence is "inappreciable or imperceptible."[^8]

[^1]: Charles Comte, *Traité de Législation: VOL II*, ch. 7 [translation](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/).

[^2]: Ibid., [p-17c4d8](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-17c4d8).

[^3]: Ibid., [p-8ecf40](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-8ecf40), [p-29b32e](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-29b32e).

[^4]: Ibid., [p-8ecf40](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-8ecf40), [p-29b32e](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-29b32e).

[^5]: Ibid., [p-3d4de8](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-3d4de8).

[^6]: Ibid., [p-07671d](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-07671d), [p-38f187](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-38f187), [p-f7a457](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-f7a457).

[^7]: Ibid., [p-3a07f6](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-3a07f6).

[^8]: Ibid., [p-fbba4b](https://falsafa.corpus/works/charles-comte-traite-de-legislation-vol-ii-20331d/07-du-developpement-physique-acquis-en-amerique-et-da/translation/#p-fbba4b).
